# UC-18.1.7 · Cisco APIC Cluster Health — infraCluster Consensus, Replica Backlog, and Controller Leadership Telemetry

## Description

Splunk monitors Cisco APIC controller-plane clustering via Managed Objects **infraCluster**, **infraClusterState**, **cluster_health**, **infraSnNode** (serial-to-node binding), **infraWiNode** (**fabric_work_id**), and **topology/pod-{id}/node-{id}/sys** rows distinguishing **fabricNode** roles (**controller** versus leaf/spine). Audit trails (**aaaModLR**) expose deliberate membership edits alongside VMware-backed VM-APIC footprints where hypervisor disk pressure shadows **infrap-rdb** latency. The replicated policy database behaves like **ZooKeeper** consensus atop controller **appliance** shards: **ifabric** leadership cycles, shard rebalance, **replicas_pending** growth, **db_size** momentum, **replicas_synced** gaps, **replication-delay**, **db_health**, and drive posture foreshadow quorum loss, asymmetric leadership, or **MOM** version drift during **ISSU**. Fault codes **F0214**, **F1394**, **F1426**, **F0951**, **F606395** corroborate syslog when REST polls lag—signals orthogonal to tenant **healthInst** dashboards.

## Value

Three-plus APIC nodes must maintain replicated consensus before tenants inherit stable contracts—pending replicas or drifting firmware silently amplify commit latency until ISSU windows collide with automation bursts. Splunk stitches REST-fed cluster telemetry with mnemonic syslog so bridges escalate controller-plane divergence before APIC CLI catches asymmetric leadership cycles.

## Implementation

**Quick win:** Hourly alert when **`replicas_pending_max>=8`** OR **`leader_events>=2`** inside **`win_bin`** on **`index=cisco_aci`** **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:cluster`** exposes quorum strain **. **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (**Splunkbase** **4022**) handles **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog`** while HTTPS collectors authenticated via **`aaaLogin.json`**/**`aaaRefresh.json`** emit **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:cluster`** lines sourced from **`/api/node/class/infraCluster.json`**, **`cluster_health.json`**, **`infraWiNode.json`**, **`infraSnNode.json`**, and **`topology/pod-*/node-*/sys.json`** plus Insights **`GET`** **`https://<nd-insights>/sedgeapi/v1/site_status`** pairing **`site_code`** lookups **.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
Production Cisco APIC clusters on supported 6.0(x)/6.1(x) trains expose HTTPS 443/tcp endpoints—provision automation identities isolated from GUI admins because overlapping credentials collide with aaaRefresh lifecycles. Install Splunk_TA_cisco-aci (Splunkbase 4022) wherever syslog terminates so **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog`** retains mnemonic corroboration while complementary HTTPS collectors emit **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:cluster`** payloads rooted in **`infraCluster`**, **`infraClusterState`**, and **`cluster_health`** Managed Objects. Layer Splunk REST API Modular Input (rest_ta, Splunkbase 1546) or hardened Python modular scripts executing POST aaaLogin.json, POST aaaRefresh.json, followed by GET /api/node/class/infraCluster.json, GET /api/node/class/cluster_health.json, GET /api/node/class/infraWiNode.json, GET /api/node/class/infraSnNode.json, plus parameterized GET /api/node/mo/topology/pod-{pod}/node-{node}/sys.json pulls tying fabricNode roles (controller versus leaf/spine) beneath topology/pod-* hierarchies. Mirror Nexus Dashboard Insights exports via GET https://<nd-insights>/sedgeapi/v1/site_status so Splunk merges APIC timelines with ND-wide site_code posture widgets. Capacity planners must budget indexer EPS whenever infraWiNode fabric_work_id churn spikes—those bursts multiply shards stored inside infrap-rdb partitions hosted on controller appliance VMs sharing ZooKeeper-style consensus semantics across three or more APIC peers before MOM versions converge during ISSU trains.

Shard rebalancing events across appliance VMs occasionally ripple replicas_pending counters without syslog amplification—still correlate Splunk timelines with Cisco Visore renders plus CLI cluster status transcripts before blaming VMware-only narratives.

Step 1 — Data sources
Schedule REST collectors polling infraCluster aggregates alongside cluster_health snapshots every 60–300 seconds, translating APIC attributes into **`index=cisco_aci`** **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:cluster`** rows capturing **oper_state**, **admin_state**, **leader**, **replicas_pending**, **replicas_synced**, **db_health**, **db_size**, **version**, **drives_state**, **fault_code**, **fault_severity**, **last_state_change_ts**. Parallel syslog pipelines steer APIC facility=local6 frames through Splunk_TA_cisco-aci so mnemonic fault escalations (**F0214**, **F1394**, **F1426**, **F0951**, **F606395**) stay visible inside **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog`** `_raw` text whenever REST polls lag interactive faults. Where Cisco publishes supplemental **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:health`** or **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault`** extracts inside the same TA footprint, preserve distinct sourcetypes rather than collapsing transports—Splunk dedup macros rely on sourcetype fidelity when correlating fault_code mixes versus cluster_health replicas_synced deltas. Insights site_status JSON complements APIC-only narratives especially when Nexus Dashboard aggregates multi-site fabrics Splunk scopes through site_code lookups.

Step 2 — Field extractions
After running **`fieldsummary`** against baseline JSON samples, codify **`FIELDALIAS`** macros so **`dn`**, **`name`**, **`id`**, **`oper_state`**, **`admin_state`**, **`version`**, **`replicas_synced`**, **`replicas_pending`**, **`leader`**, **`fabric_work_id`**, **`db_health`**, **`db_size`**, **`drives_state`**, **`fault_code`**, **`fault_severity`**, **`last_state_change_ts`** align despite camelCase drift post TA upgrades. Map CIM Inventory semantics (**host**, **hostname**, **vendor**, **version**, **family**) by enriching infraSnNode.serial bindings tying hardware identities to topology/pod-* nodes while distinguishing fabricNode role attributes (controller versus leaf/spine/tier-2-leaf). Map CIM Performance gauges (**cpu_load_percent**, **mem_used_percent**) only when adjunct procCpu/procMem scrapes accompany topology/pod-*/node-*/sys snapshots describing apic VMs.

Step 3 — Initial validation
Cross-check Splunk timelines versus acidiag avread counters gathered inside restricted APIC shells alongside cluster status transcripts emitted via APIC CLI. Align dashboards against Nexus Dashboard Insights controller redundancy widgets plus Visore renders anchored at infraCluster distinguished names—Splunk deviations wider than twice the collector cadence hint duplicated transports or stale APIC-cookie rotation.

Step 4 — SPL building blocks
Normalize identities using **`eval`** helpers (**dn_lc**, **wi_id**, **replicas_pending_num**, **fault_code_u**, **leader_lc**, **sw_ver**) before **`sort`** **dn_lc**, **wi_id**, **_time** sequences feeding **`streamstats`** window arguments watching leader_chg bursts and oper_flap oscillations sourced from infraWiNode fabric_work_id churn. Aggregate using **`stats`** keyed **dn_lc**, **wi_id**, **win_bin** summarizing **leader_events**, **replicas_pending_max**, **fault_distinct**. Layer **`lookup`** aci_controller_inventory on **dn_lc** prior to collapsing timelines so **site_code** survives **`stats`** rollups **by** **win_bin**. Collapse controllers using nested **`stats`** calculating **dc(ctrl_sw)** version_skew alongside **dc(dn_lc)** controllers_seen before **`eval`** **quorum_warn** compares controllers_seen against three-controller quorum expectations tied to replicas_pending_max spikes mirroring replication-delay telemetry.

Splunk operators must rehearse **`bin`**, **`sort`**, **`streamstats`**, **`stats`**, **`lookup`**, **`eval`**, **`where`**, **`head`**, and **`table`** stages whenever upgrading TA revisions—camelCase drift silently nullifies replicas_pending_num parsing until FIELDALIAS refreshes propagate across search heads.

Step 5 — Comprehensive enrichment patterns
Fuse Insights site_status responses with Splunk site_code lookups bridging ND remediation narratives versus infraCluster timelines. Blend nightly CMDB extracts tagging owning_team plus apic_serial anchors aligning infraSnNode.serial metadata. Harmonize ServiceNow CHG CSV drops referencing ISSU bundles against aaaModLR auditUser identities stamping deliberate membership edits—distinct from UC-18.3.8 multi-vendor SDN audits yet equally authoritative for Standby APIC workflows. Subscribe openVuln REST feeds surfacing Cisco PSIRT notices correlated with ctrl_sw telemetry hashes when version drift persists beyond ISSU soak timers. Annotate forensic escalations referencing MITRE T1495 firmware compromise framing whenever version_skew coincides with suspicious fault_code bursts unrelated to dataplane anomalies. Tie Cisco TAC SR extracts exposing still-open investigations keyed by apic rack identifiers embedded inside infraSnNode metadata. Attach Splunk ITSI services modeling APIC cluster KPI parents cascading beneath ACI fabric dependencies terminating at tenant workloads.

Replication-delay gauges exported beside replicas_pending deltas deserve spotlight dashboards because asymmetric delays precede outright quorum loss whenever controller appliance disks contend with VMware snapshot merges during standby rehearsals or cold standby promotions staged outside CAB spreadsheets. Disk and inode pressure against infrap-rdb mount points amplifies db_size derivatives Splunk plots—even without syslog floods—because shard compaction stalls inflate replicas_pending faster than ISSU planners anticipate whenever ESXi datastores briefly pause IO.

aaaModLR audits reveal deliberate membership churn distinct from dataplane faults; correlate Splunk aaaModLR extracts with infraWiNode fabric_work_id sequencing so ISSU planners reconcile Splunk quorum_warn positives against scripted Standby APIC drills rather than fabric-wide outages alone.

Step 6 — Operational runbook
Fan out cluster-degraded Sev-1 escalation macros referencing risk_core predicates blending quorum_warn, version_skew, leader_events, replicas_pending_max. ISSU throttle governance aligns CAB spreadsheets suppressing benign bursts tied to staged Standby APIC rehearsals. Standby APIC promotion workflows cite cold standby activation sequences mirrored inside aaaModLR transcripts plus APIC CLI ifc leadership recovery scripts when leader churn persists beyond scripted maintenance windows. Operational MTBF objectives exceed 2160 hours between Sev-1-worthy regressions excluding scripted rehearsals. Operational MTTR commitments remain below 75 minutes measured from risk_core positives through acknowledgement timestamps logged inside ticketing bridges. Dashboard wiring binds tok_site, tok_pod, tok_leader tokens bridging leader-election timelines with fault_code top N charts, cluster oper-state matrices, replication latency plots derived from db_size derivatives, version drift histograms sourced from ctrl_sw mixes, plus syslog overlays validating mnemonic fault_code escalations.

Troubleshooting modes remain deliberately verbose because APIC clustering faults masquerade as benign syslog storms: duplicated collectors referencing identical APIC VIP addresses double-count replicas_pending until collector_id metadata partitions KPI denominators. HTTP 429 saturation surfaces whenever infraWiNode pulls widen rsp-subtree scopes—relax intervals or shard MO classes across modular inputs. aaaRefresh misses mimic calm fabrics despite climbing fault_distinct counters inside cluster_health extracts—inspect Heavy Forwarder splunkd.log pathways prior to rewriting SPL. leader_chg bursts coinciding solely with scripted Standby drills warrant CAB correlation rather than Sev-1 paging alone. mvcount anomalies tied to fw_seen mixes occasionally spike when ctrl_sw strings embed whitespace—normalize via trim eval helpers before dc stats math collapses firmware tuples.

Governance tip: whenever Splunk surfaces quorum_warn positives alongside Insights green LEDs, escalate jointly—network telemetry alone rarely exposes APIC consensus regressions hiding beneath dataplane greens while tenants continue forwarding packets uninterrupted.

Splunk Risk-Based Alerting adapters may stitch quorum_warn positives into ticketing bridges referencing owning_team lookups refreshed nightly so escalation desks inherit CMDB certainty during Sev-1 bridges.



## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_aci (sourcetype="cisco:aci:cluster" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog") earliest=-6h@m
| eval dn_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(dn, infraCluster_dn, distinguished_name, "")))
| eval wi_id=trim(coalesce(id, infraWiNode_id, wi_node_id, ""))
| eval oper_state_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(oper_state, operState, "")))
| eval replicas_pending_num=tonumber(coalesce(replicas_pending, replicasPending, "0"))
| eval replicas_synced_num=tonumber(coalesce(replicas_synced, replicasSynced, "0"))
| eval fault_code_u=upper(trim(coalesce(fault_code, faultCode, code, "")))
| eval leader_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(leader, cluster_leader, "")))
| eval sw_ver=trim(coalesce(version, sw_version, firmware_version, ""))
| eval syslog_hit=if(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(F0214|F1394|F1426|F0951|F606395|cluster|leader|quorum|replica|rdb|split|standby)"),1,0)
| eval cluster_row=if(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:cluster",1,syslog_hit)
| where cluster_row>0
| fields _time host dn_lc wi_id oper_state_lc replicas_pending_num replicas_synced_num fault_code_u leader_lc sw_ver sourcetype
| sort dn_lc wi_id _time
| streamstats window=18 global=f current=f latest(leader_lc) as prev_leader latest(oper_state_lc) as prev_oper latest(sw_ver) as prev_ver by dn_lc wi_id
| eval leader_chg=if(isnotnull(prev_leader) AND prev_leader!=leader_lc AND len(leader_lc)>0,1,0)
| eval oper_flap=if(isnotnull(prev_oper) AND prev_oper!=oper_state_lc AND len(oper_state_lc)>0,1,0)
| eval ver_chg=if(isnotnull(prev_ver) AND prev_ver!=sw_ver AND len(sw_ver)>0,1,0)
| bin _time span=5m as win_bin
| stats sum(leader_chg) as leader_events sum(oper_flap) as oper_events sum(ver_chg) as ver_events max(replicas_pending_num) as replicas_pending_max latest(replicas_synced_num) as replicas_synced_latest dc(fault_code_u) as fault_distinct values(fault_code_u) as fault_vals latest(sw_ver) as ctrl_sw by dn_lc wi_id win_bin
| lookup aci_controller_inventory dn_lc OUTPUT site_code owning_team standby_hint
| stats max(replicas_pending_max) as replicas_pending_max sum(leader_events) as leader_events sum(oper_events) as oper_events sum(ver_events) as ver_events max(fault_distinct) as fault_distinct values(fault_vals) as fault_vals dc(ctrl_sw) as version_skew values(ctrl_sw) as fw_seen dc(dn_lc) as controllers_seen values(dn_lc) as dn_seen values(site_code) as site_codes values(owning_team) as teams values(standby_hint) as standby_hints by win_bin
| eval quorum_warn=if(controllers_seen<3 AND replicas_pending_max>0,1,0)
| eval risk_core=replicas_pending_max>=6 OR leader_events>=2 OR quorum_warn=1 OR version_skew>1 OR oper_events>=4 OR fault_distinct>=2
| where risk_core=1 OR replicas_pending_max>=3 OR leader_events>=1
| sort - replicas_pending_max leader_events
| head 200
| table win_bin replicas_pending_max leader_events oper_events quorum_warn version_skew fault_vals fw_seen dn_seen controllers_seen site_codes teams standby_hints
```

## Visualization

(1) Matrix comparing oper_state cohorts per dn_lc controller row with leader badges. (2) Line timechart plotting replicas_pending_max versus db_size derivatives per win_bin. (3) Column chart highlighting version_skew counts sourced from ctrl_sw mixes. (4) Top fault_code statistics paired with syslog excerpts. (5) Timeline layering leader_events spikes atop risk_core overlays.
